page.content: In a study involving a total of 119 babies, trauma incidents were categorized with notable occurrences including scalp loss with a count of 5, laceration over eye with a count of 3, NNU admission with a count of 1, and neonatal death (sepsis) with a count of 1, culminating in a total of 10 cases, representing 8.4% of the population. Additionally, in the administration of virus titer measured in log10 PFU/organ across skin and brain, the control group exhibited a skin virus titer of 6.1 ± 0.07 and a brain virus titer of 2.2 ± 0.02, while AF-06 demonstrated a skin virus titer of 6.3 ± 0.11 and a brain virus titer of 1.6 ± 0.48.

lines.text: In a study involving a total of 119 babies, trauma incidents were categorized with notable occurrences including scalp loss with
a count of 5, laceration over eye with a count of 3, NNU admission with a count of 1, and neonatal death (sepsis) with a count
of 1, culminating in a total of 10 cases, representing 8.4% of the population. Additionally, in the administration of virus titer
measured in log10 PFU/organ across skin and brain, the control group exhibited a skin virus titer of 6.1 ± 0.07 and a brain virus
titer of 2.2 ± 0.02, while AF-06 demonstrated a skin virus titer of 6.3 ± 0.11 and a brain virus titer of 1.6 ± 0.48.

page.content: The AF-07 group revealed a skin virus titer of 5.7 ± 0.09* and a brain virus titer of <1.0 ± 0.00**, with AF-08 showing a skin virus titer of 6.1 ± 0.04 and a brain virus titer of <1.0 ± 0.00**. The ACV (5 mg/kg) treatment resulted in a skin virus titer of 5.4 ± 0.14* and a brain virus titer of <1.0 ± 0.00**.

virus titer of 6.1 ± 0.04 and a brain virus titer of <1.0 ± 0.00**. The ACV (5 mg/kg) treatment resulted in a skin virus titer of 5.4
± 0.14* and a brain virus titer of <1.0 ± 0.00**.
